NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 301, PER RECUSAL OF JUDGE MAHONEY. MATTER TO BE HEARD ON AUGUST 2, 2007. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR #11332

LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF AND CROSS DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA'S, N.A. (USA) DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO STATE A CLAIM UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW IF DEFENDANT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. UNDER FEDERAL LAW, PLAINTIFF AS THE CREDITOR IS NOT A DEBT COLLECTOR OR SUBJECT TO THE F D C P A EVEN FOR ACTS OF ITS AGENTS. (SEE THOMASSON VS. BANK ONE, LOUISIANA (E.D. LA 2001) 137 F. SUPP 2D 721, 723; MEADS VS. CITY CORP CREDIT SERVICES (S.D. GA 1998) 668 F SUPP 330, 333-4). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR 8189
